core_mechanism: multi-timescale differential (delta) encoding of a temporally-correlated state sequence relative to periodically-refreshed reference states, using a two-level reference hierarchy so the current state stays recoverable from a coarse reference plus available fine increments even when some increments are lost
brief_reason: The abstract exploits temporal correlation between consecutive global models to transmit differences instead of full states, and adds a second, coarser reference timescale so a lost differential update can still be reconstructed between full broadcasts — a hierarchical delta-coding scheme with error-resilient reference refresh.
